meta_title: For Freelancers: client workflow to Solve slow development speed (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome slow development speed. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelance Workflow: Fix Slow Development with AI Tools
content.seo_description: Stop manual coding bottlenecks. Learn an intermediate AI-driven client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship SaaS products at record speeds.
content.answer_block: To eliminate slow development, move from manual boilerplate coding to an AI-orchestrated workflow. By integrating Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative CLI-based feature implementation, you can reduce your time-to-market by 70%, allowing you to focus on high-value client logic rather than repetitive syntax.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your sprint. You’re buried in boilerplate, wrestling with environment configurations, and the client is asking for a feature that would normally take three days of heavy lifting. This isn't just a productivity dip; it's a bottleneck that kills your reputation as a high-velocity freelancer.

But the game has changed. By shifting from 'manual developer' to 'AI architect,' you can strip away the friction that slows you down. This guide outlines a professional-grade workflow that leverages Antigravity and Claude Code to turn hours of tedious coding into minutes of strategic execution, ensuring you deliver enterprise-level software at startup speed.

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding
steps.description: Stop starting from scratch. Use Antigravity to generate your base project structure with pre-configured best practices and security standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready Next.js SaaS scaffold with Tailwind CSS, Supabase authentication, and a Stripe payment integration pattern.

steps.title: Context-Aware Feature Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to read your local codebase and suggest implementation paths for new features based on existing design patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project structure and propose a clean, modular pattern for adding a new user dashboard widget.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze the current architecture and implement a modular dashboard component for user analytics.'

steps.title: Automated Code Refactoring
steps.description: Clean up technical debt and improve performance by letting the AI refactor complex legacy components into modern, readable code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the existing authentication middleware to be more efficient and secure, adhering to current TypeScript best practices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the auth middleware in src/middleware.ts for performance and security.'

steps.title: Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Ensure reliability before the client sees the work. Automate the creation of unit tests for every new feature you push.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate comprehensive Jest test suites for the newly created user dashboard component, covering edge cases and error states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Write unit tests for the dashboard component using Jest and React Testing Library.'

steps.title: Deployment Orchestration
steps.description: Streamline the CI/CD pipeline by using AI to configure your environment variables and deployment scripts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow file that automates testing and deployment to Vercel upon code merge.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a .github/workflows/deploy.yml file for automated Vercel deployment.'

pro_tips: Always define a 'System Persona' in your Antigravity config file to ensure all generated code consistently matches your preferred coding style and library choices.
Use Claude Code's `--memory` flag to maintain state across complex multi-file refactors, preventing the AI from losing track of your project's specific conventions.
Before finalizing a feature, ask the AI to 'Critique this code for potential security vulnerabilities' to catch common oversights before they reach production.
Create a 'Project Knowledge Base' markdown file and point Claude Code to it so the AI understands your specific client requirements and business constraints.
Never blindly copy AI output; use the CLI diff tools to review changes step-by-step, maintaining mastery over your codebase while gaining speed.
content.next_steps: After implementing this workflow, audit your next project's timeline to measure the reduction in 'Time-to-Feature.' Experiment by adding an AI-driven PR review process to your workflow to further automate quality assurance.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your tools and start building the future. Your clients deserve faster delivery, and you deserve a workflow that doesn't burn you out. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
